solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String gcdOfStrings(String str1, String str2) {
        if (!(str1 + str2).equals(str2 + str1)) {
            return "";
        }
        int gcdLength = gcd(str1.length(), str2.length());
        return str1.substring(0, gcdLength);
    }

    private int gcd(int a, int b) {
        while (b != 0) {
            int temp = b;
            b = a % b;
            a = temp;
        }
        return a;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n + m)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution checks if the concatenation of the two strings in both orders is equal, ensuring a common repeating pattern. If so, it calculates the greatest common divisor (GCD) of their lengths and returns the substring of that length from the first string. This approach is efficient and leverages mathematical properties of GCD for optimal performance.>
